title: routing.c: SRH parser ignores CmprI/CmprE compression fields
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/routing.c:115-154

lichen_rpl_srh_parse() ignores the CmprI (data[2]) and CmprE (data[3]) compression fields from RFC 6554 Section 3. The parser assumes all addresses are uncompressed (16 bytes each) based on the check at line 130:

```c
size_t addr_bytes = len - 6;
if (addr_bytes % 16 != 0) {
    return LICHEN_RPL_ERR_TOO_SHORT;
}
```

If a received SRH uses address compression (CmprI or CmprE > 0), the parser will:
1. Miscompute the number of addresses
2. Parse wrong byte boundaries for each address
3. Return incorrect routing information

Impact:
- Packets with compressed SRHs would be misrouted
- No memory safety issue (just incorrect address values)
- Low risk in LICHEN's closed ecosystem where root generates SRHs with CmprI=CmprE=0

Fix: Validate compression fields are zero, reject if not:
```c
if (data[2] != 0 || data[3] != 0) {
    return LICHEN_RPL_ERR_BAD_RT;  /* Compression not supported */
}
```
close_reason: Fixed: Added CmprI/CmprE validation in both C (routing.c) and Rust (routing.rs) SRH parsers to reject compressed SRHs which would be misrouted
